items.definition_en: A contract is a written or spoken agreement between two or more people.
items.example_en: We signed a contract for the new job.

items.definition_en: An agreement is when people decide together on something.
items.example_en: We reached an agreement after a long discussion.

items.definition_en: A signature is your name written by yourself.
items.example_en: He put his signature at the end of the contract.

items.definition_en: A partnership is when two or more people work together.
items.example_en: They started a new partnership last month.

items.definition_en: A clause is a special part of a contract or agreement.
items.example_en: This clause explains the payment rules.

items.definition_en: A promise is when someone says they will do something.
items.example_en: He made a promise to help with the work.

items.definition_en: A condition is something that must be met in an agreement.
items.example_en: One condition is that we finish on time.

items.definition_en: A signature page is the last part of a contract where people sign.
items.example_en: Please add your name to the signature page.

items.definition_en: A witness is someone who sees and confirms an agreement.
items.example_en: She asked her friend to be a witness.

items.definition_en: A document is a paper with important information or agreement.
items.example_en: The lawyer checked the document carefully.

items.definition_en: A negotiation is a discussion to reach an agreement.
items.example_en: They had a negotiation to decide the price.

items.definition_en: Approval is when something is officially accepted.
items.example_en: We need your approval before we start.

items.definition_en: A party is one person or group in an agreement.
items.example_en: Each party must follow the rules.

items.definition_en: A commitment is a promise to do something.
items.example_en: She made a commitment to deliver on time.
